data.description:
## Problem
The `[rebar:<id>]` citation section in `docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md` (shipped by 266e-c8b6-0cb0-44ae) states the edge requirement (this ticket `depends_on -> <id>`, OR `<id> blocks -> this ticket`) but never says the edge must be DIRECT. The shipped verifier `det_citation._edge_verified` checks only the plan ticket's OWN direct deps plus the cited ticket's direct `blocks` (no recursion), so a transitive/indirect dependency does NOT satisfy it — the citation then reads as edge-unbacked and grounds as normal (fails closed) with no warning in the doc. This footgun was hit in practice (a ticket transitively depending on a config ticket still needed a direct edge).

## Approach (docs-only; no behavior change)
Add one clause to the EXISTING citation section stating the edge must be DIRECT and that a transitive/indirect dependency does not count.

## Grounding (verified via Read)
- CONFIRMED `docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md` contains the "## Citing a prerequisite's symbol (`[rebar:<id>]`)" section with the edge-requirement paragraph.
- CONFIRMED `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_citation.py` `_edge_verified` checks only direct edges (the plan ticket's own `deps` for `depends_on -> C`, and `resolve_deps(C)` for `blocks -> P`) — no recursive closure — so "direct" is accurate to the shipped behavior.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] The citation section explicitly states the upstream edge must be DIRECT and that a transitive/indirect dependency does not satisfy it. PROVE: `grep -Eiq "direct" docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md && grep -Eiq "transitive|indirect" docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md`.
- [ ] No behavioral/code change; docs only. PROVE: `git diff --name-only origin/main | grep -qxE "docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md"` and no other files changed.

## Files likely touched
- docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md

## Estimated scope: XS
data.title: writing-a-passing-plan: [rebar:<id>] citations require a DIRECT edge (transitive deps do not count)
